DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: WITHIN 14 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANT CTOUR HOLIDAY LLC. SHALL (I) SERVE A FURTHER RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY (SET ONE) NO. 2 THAT FULLY COMPLIES WITH CCP 2030.220 AND 2030.240; (II) SERVE FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION (SET ONE) NOS. 1, 13, 14, 16, 18, 19, 21 AND 22 THAT FULLY COMPLY WITH CCP 2031.220 AND 2031.240; AND (III) AND PAY PLAINTIFF DISCOVERY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,363. TO THE EXTENT THE MOTION SEEKS AN ORDER COMPELLING PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE IDENTIFIED REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, IT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS PREMATURE. THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED THE UNTIMELY DECLARATION OF MR. NORMAN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROGER MEAD; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative Evidence And Issue Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. MATTHEW MIHALY, ESQ. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-262-8535) appearing for plaintiff;
Atty. RONALD E. NORMAN, ESQ. of Law Offices of Farnell & Norman (Tel: 949-553-1300) appearing for defendant.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LASSEN TOUR & TRAVEL, INC.'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative Evidence And Issue Sanctions.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. Both sides stipulate to the pro tem judge. Matter argued. The Court instructs plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion To Enforce Prior Order To Compel Discovery And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David E. Russo, Esq., Lewis Brisbois, 415-438-6629, appearing for Plaintiff;
Ronald E. Norman, Esq., Farnell & Norman, 949-553-1300, appearing for Defendants;

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LASSEN TOUR & TRAVEL, INC.'S Motion To Enforce Prior Order To Compel Discovery And Monetary Sanctions is taken under submission. Judge Pro Tem: Tony Schoenberg; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Sep-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID E. RUSSO, ESQ. of Lewis Brisbois (Tel: 415-438-6629) appearing for plaintiff Lassen Tour & Travel, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LASSEN TOUR & TRAVEL, INC.'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion is granted as follows: Within 14 days of entry of this order, Defendant CTour Holiday LLC. shall (i) serve a further response to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatory (Set One) No. 2 that fully complies with CCP 2030.220 and 2030.240; (ii) serve further responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production (Set One) Nos. 1, 13, 14, 16, 18, 19, 21 and 22 that fully comply with CCP 2031.220 and 2031.240; and (iii) and pay Plaintiff discovery sanctions in the amount of $3,363. To the extent the motion seeks an order compelling production of documents responsive to the identified Requests for Production, it is denied without prejudice as premature. The Court has considered the untimely declaration of Mr. Norman.

Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)
